{{Quote|For years, you've watched the greatest Star Wars battles. What if you could actually live them?|Tagline|<ref name="SW:BF" />}}
'''''Star Wars: Battlefront''''' is a third/first-person shooter [[Star Wars video games|video game]] based around battles featured in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' movies. It was developed by [[Pandemic Studios]] and [[LucasArts]], and released on [[September 21]], [[2004]]<ref name="Year by Year" /> for [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] (playable on [[Wikipedia:Xbox 360|Xbox 360]]), [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]], the same day as the release of the [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|''Star Wars'' Trilogy DVD set]]. Players can play ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' either online, or offline with up to four players (on consoles).

A sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', was released on [[November 1]], [[2005]], and a compilation including both games, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront Classic Collection]]'', was released on [[March 14]], [[2024]]. The [[Star Wars Battlefront (series)|game series]] also featured several spin-offs, and a reboot, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront]]'', released on [[November 17]], [[2015]].

==Plot summary==
The Historical Campaign in ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' is divided into two parts, the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], following the timeline of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films.
===Clone Wars Campaign===
*'''[[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]]''' ([[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]): The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] has [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|blockaded]] the peaceful planet of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The Trade Federation army of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] marches to the [[Great Grass Plains/Legends|Great Grass Plains]] to destroy the native [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]], which opposes them on the surface. The Trade Federation army arrives in [[Multi-Troop Transport/Legends|Multi-Troop Transports]] with [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tanks]] (AATs). The Gungan Grand Army awaits them with legions of [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] soldiers, some riding [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]]. Their [[Fambaa/Legends|fambaas]] arrive with giant portable [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|deflector shield generators]], which are activated to protect the Gungan army. The attacking Trade Federation force overwhelms the shielding and destroys the generators, allowing the AATs to tear through the Gungan force.
*'''Rebellion on Theed''' (32 BBY): The people and [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|security forces]] of the Naboo capital city of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] attack the Trade Federation's army of battle droids, supported by [[P-series droideka]], in an attempt to assist [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]] in recapturing the planet. Despite their efforts, the Trade Federation kills all the Royal Guards trying to defend the city.
*'''[[Kashyyyk Trade Rebellion|Aggressive Negotiations]]''' ([[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]]): The [[Wookiee Trade Guild]] resists the taxes imposed by the Trade Federation, which is now part of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] (CIS). In retaliation the CIS sends [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious's]] new apprentice [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and an army of battle droids, P-series droideka, and the new [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] to end the conflict. The CIS force makes a direct strike at the headquarters of the Trade Guild on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. The CIS successfully destroys the Wookiee Trade Guild and its private army.
*'''The [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]]''' ([[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]):[[File:Two_Republic_gunships_in_Star_Wars_Battlefront.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|LAATs]] on Geonosis]] The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] has just acquired a new [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] from the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. The [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] army arrives at the Separatist base on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] with Jedi Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] to destroy a Separatist army made up of battle droids, super battle droids, [[W-series droideka/Legends|W-series droideka]], and [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosians]]. The clone army uses their [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TEs]] to destroy the [[Hardcell-class interstellar transport/Legends|Techno Union ships]], which the CIS was using to evacuate, and the clone troopers kill most of the Geonosian combatants and destroy the droid army.
*'''[[First Battle of Kamino|Assault on Kamino]]''' (22 BBY): The CIS has discovered the cloning facilities on Kamino and has sent an army of battle droids, super battle droids, and W-series droideka to destroy them. The Republic clone army left to defend it destroys all of the attacking droids.
*'''[[Battle of Rhen Var (Clone Wars)|Mountaintop Defenses]]''' (22 BBY): The Republic has discovered a long-range sensor station on [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]] created by Count Dooku. They send an army of clone troopers with AT-TEs and a few [[TX-130 Saber-class fighter tank/Legends|TX-130 Saber-class fighter tanks]] to Rhen Var to capture the station. The army of clones destroys all of the battle droids, super battle droids, W-series droideka, and AATs defending the facility in conjunction with another operation led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].
*'''The [[Battle of Kashyyyk/Legends|Battle of Kashyyyk]]''' ([[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]): The CIS has launches a desperate attack on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Kashyyyk. A clone army of the Republic is sent to Kashyyyk to help defend against the army of battle droids, super battle droids, droideka, [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droids]], and AATs. They successfully destroy the entire Separatist army in the battle and save the Wookiees.

===Galactic Civil War Campaign===
([[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] – [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]])
*'''[[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Desert Extermination]]''' (0 BBY): The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] sends a legion of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] with vehicular support to the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to eliminate [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] and hostile natives. The stormtrooper [[Legion/Legends|legion]] slaughters all the Rebels and [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] they find in the Dune Sea.
*'''Siege of Mos Eisley''' (0 BBY): The stormtroopers of the Empire and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] have begun searching the town of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on Tatooine for the droids which carried the plans to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. The droids have escaped, so the stormtroopers and Darth Vader eliminate all locals that are suspected of having helped the droids flee.
*'''[[Sabotage at Rhen Var]]''' (0 BBY): The Rebellion has discovered an Imperial listening outpost on the planet Rhen Var and sends a company of troopers to capture the station. The Rebels fortify the captured outpost while the Empire prepares a counterattack. A force of stormtroopers assault the station and successfully defeat the Rebels, although the outpost is destroyed.
*'''[[Raid on Yavin 4]]''' (0 BBY): The Empire sends down a legion of stormtroopers to verify the presence of a Rebel base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. They eliminate all of the Rebels in the [[Massassi Arena]], thereby verifying the location of a Rebel base.
*'''[[Evacuation of Yavin|Fall of Yavin 4]]''' (0 ABY): The Death Star has been [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroyed]] and several escape pods have crashed down on Yavin 4. The surviving stormtroopers attack the Rebel base but are all killed by the Rebels defending the base.
*'''The [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]''' ([[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]): The [[Echo Base/Legends|Rebel Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] has been discovered by the Empire, and stormtroopers and [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] (AT-ATs) are sent down to destroy the Rebel base, protected by a shield generator, before the Rebels can evacuate. The Rebels, with the help of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], destroy the AT-ATs and prevent the stormtroopers from attacking long enough so that the transports could escape.
*'''[[Battle of Bespin (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Cloud City]]''' (Sometime 3-4 ABY): The Rebellion sends a company of Rebels to attack the Imperial garrison on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. The Rebels then eliminate the Imperial forces in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]].
*'''Battle in the Clouds''' (Sometime 3-4 ABY): Following the liberation of Cloud City, the Rebellion moves to eliminate all of the stormtroopers on the main mining platform on Bespin.
*'''The [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]''' (4 ABY): The second [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] is being constructed above the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. The Rebellion sends a legion of Rebels which allied with the native Ewoks to destroy the [[Endor shield generator bunker|shield bunker]] which projects a shield around the second Death Star. The Rebels succeed in the mission, allowing for the destruction of the battlestation.

===Galactic Conquest===
Galactic Conquest allows the player to choose a faction and use it to conquer the galaxy. As a result, it is considered [[Canon|non-canon]].
#[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] - Birth of the Rebellion
#[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] - [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark Side]] Rising
#[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] - Attack of the [[Cloning/Legends|Clones]]
#[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|CIS]] - Revenge of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]
#Rebels/Empire - The Galactic Civil War
#Rebels/Empire - A Galaxy Divided
#Republic/CIS - The Clone Wars
#Republic/CIS - Balance of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]

==Gameplay==
===Character overview===
''Star Wars: Battlefront'' lets players control a soldier in a first- or third-person perspective. There are also vehicles players can jump into, both ground and airborne.<ref name="SW:BF" />

The game features four main factions from both the [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]] and the [[Original trilogy|classic]] trilogies. They are the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] (Separatists) of the prequel era, and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] of the classic trilogy. However, factions can only play their historical adversary; Rebels cannot fight Separatists, for example. Within each unit, five different classes of characters become available.<ref name="SW:BF" />

Four of the classes are fairly similar for each faction: a basic infantry soldier, a heavy weapons trooper with a rocket launcher, a pilot, and a [[Sniper/Legends|sniper]]. The fifth class is different for each faction. The Imperials have the [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]], with a jump pack which enables short but high jumps, plus a blast cannon, a pistol and grenades. The Republic has the [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|jet trooper]], with a jetpack, which enables long but low jumps, an enhanced pistol, grenades and an [[Electromagnetic pulse launcher|EMP launcher]]. The Separatists have the [[Droideka/Legends|droideka]], which is very powerful, fast, and equipped with a personal shield, but can't use vehicles. The Rebels have the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler]], with extra health, time bombs, a bowcaster, and a grenade launcher. Players choose their class at the spawn screen.<ref name="SW:BF" />

[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] heroes are featured in the game: the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] gets [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] wields his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] for the Empire, [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] fights along with the Republic, and [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] is with the Separatists. Other Jedi characters appear but are not playable; they may only be the player's allies in battle, under certain circumstances. The heroes will occasionally fight alongside the player in the campaign battles, in which case the other team won't have a hero. Heroes can be enabled in Instant Action mode, allowing both teams to have a Jedi hero. Once his team's reinforcement count drops below a certain number, the hero automatically dies.<ref name="SW:BF" />

There are also non-playable characters (NPCs): [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] roam the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and attack both sides, [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] patrol the streets of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] inhabit the jungle of the forest moon of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] battle on the [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] Plain, [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal guards]] patrol Theed, [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] defend [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] and [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosians]] aid the Separatists on [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]]. Additionally, there are droids which can provide [[Power droid/Legends|ammo]] or health [[Medical droid/Legends|refills]]. Players can also take over turrets.<ref name="SW:BF" />

===Game style===
Every match sees the players fighting the enemy faction. They win by forcing the enemy reinforcement count to zero or capturing all five Command Posts spread throughout the map.<ref name="SW:BF" />

The multiplayer matches can be online or offline on any of the available maps. The single-player skirmish missions are known as Instant Action.<ref name="SW:BF" />

Players may play battles by themselves with NPCs or play an online multiplayer via Internet, LAN or direct connection. Multiplayer consists of up to 16 online players vs. players on consoles or up to 40 on PC. The servers on PC are dedicated or standard and can be protected with a password.

===Maps===
#Bespin: Platforms
#Bespin: Cloud City
#Endor: Bunker
#Geonosis: Spire

#Kamino: Tipoca City
#Kashyyyk: Docks

In addition the map Tatooine: Jabba's Palace came with the 1.2 update.

[[File:Clone_sharpshooter.jpg|thumb|250px|A [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Sniper/Legends|sharpshooter]] on Geonosis]]

===Playable vehicles and ships===
These vehicles have many capabilities from the movies; a Republic Gunship can carry/hold up to five people, and Rebel Snowspeeders can launch a [[Power harpoon launcher|tow cable]] out of the rear of the vehicle to trip AT-ATs. In addition, blaster turrets and emplaced guns are available on various maps including unique turrets in maps such as Yavin temple and Hoth.

===Unit types===
*Basic Infantryman: [[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone trooper]], [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|Super Battle Droid]], [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droid]] (Campaign only), [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel Soldier]], [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtrooper]]. They carry blasters and explosives.
*Sniper: [[Clone sharpshooter/Legends|Clone Sharpshooter]], [[Assassin droid/Legends|Assassin Droid]], [[Rebel Marksman/Legends|Rebel Marksman]], [[Scout trooper/Legends|Scout trooper]]. They carry sniper rifles and Recon Droids.
*Engineer: [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|Clone Engineer]], [[OOM pilot battle droid/Legends|Droid Engineer]], [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel Smuggler]], [[TIE pilot|Imperial Engineer]]. They carry special launchers and ammunition dispensers.
*Heavy: [[Clone heavy assault trooper|ARC Trooper]], [[Assault battle droid|Assault Droid]], [[Rebel Vanguard/Legends|Rebel vanguard]], [[Shock trooper/Legends|Shock trooper]]. They carry rocket launchers and mines.
*Special: [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|Jet Trooper]], [[Droideka/Legends|Droideka]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler]], [[Phase Zero dark trooper|Dark Trooper]]. They carry special weapons. The Jet Troopers and Dark Troopers feature jet packs, while the destroyer droids can transform into their wheeled form for increased mobility.

[[File:Imps.png|thumb|300px|Left to right: A Stormtrooper, a Shocktrooper, a Dark Trooper (Phase Zero), a Scout trooper, a Snowtrooper, an AT-AT pilot, a TIE pilot, and an AT-ST pilot]]

===Mod tools===
In late 2004, [[LucasArts]] and [[Pandemic Studios]] released the ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' BFBuilder, an unsupported [[Wikipedia:Mod (computer gaming)|modding tool]] that allows players to create new battlefields for ''Star Wars: Battlefront''.<ref name="Modding info">{{LucasArts|url=thread.jspa?threadID=20059&tstart=420|text=Thread: PC Mod Tools <nowiki>[TDE]</nowiki>|nobackup=1}}</ref> It shipped with many of the source files used to make ''Star Wars: Battlefront'', such as maps, sides, and sound configuration files; however, it did not ship with some of the files needed to make specific modifications to the main game itself (i.e. menu configuration files or font files). Two demonstration maps were also included. Sound effects and music used to make the game were not included because of copyright issues,<ref name="BFBuild Read">BFBuilder ReadMe</ref> although it is possible to import original music and sound effects into the game.

Map editing in BFBuilder is handled by a program called ZeroEdit, which is used to mold the landscapes, place objects, command posts and vehicle spawn points. Additional scripting is done through LUA files for maps, and ODF (Object Definition Files) files for objects.<ref name="ZeroEdit">{{WebCite|url=http://swbfmodding.wikidot.com/zeroeditor|text=Zeroeditor|archivedate=20191227122943}}</ref>

User-created levels and mods then go through a process called munging, which compiles assets and maps into formats readable by the game, which are then compiled into container files for in-game use. Although BFBuilder is subtly different from the [[Star Wars: Battlefront II#Mod tools|''Battlefront II'' mod tools]], assets can be imported from either one into the other.

==Development==
[[File:StarWarsBattlefrontLogo.png|thumb|right|250px|Battlefront's logo]]
On [[January 13]], [[2004]], [[LucasArts]] announced ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' as a multiplayer action game.<ref name="Press release">{{LucasArts|url=company/release/news20040113.html|text=LucasArts Announces Details On Star Wars Battlefront™}}</ref>

Development began in November [[2002]] at [[Pandemic Studios]], according to Executive Producer Greg Borrud. It was conceived as "the ultimate fan's dream." Lead Artists Dean Betton and Matt Palmer directed the art team in creating a vast array of unusual worlds and characters that have appeared in the ''Star Wars'' universe.<ref name="DevDiaries">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/videogames/news/f20040913/index.html|text=Go Behind the Scenes with Battlefront Designer Diaries}}</ref>

The developers used software called SoftImage:XSI.<ref name="DevDiary2">{{WebCite|url=http://pc.gamespy.com/pc/star-wars-battlefront/502733p1.html|text=Star Wars Battlefront – Designer Diary #2|work=GameSpy|archivedate=20161220030436}}</ref> The character models were initially modeled as static objects using a 3D modeling package in a process known as "boning and skinning." The process entails first creating a digital "skeleton." Lead programmer John Northan notes that the single-player experience is controlled by Artificial Intelligence where the AI for each entity is calculated at several different layers, ranging from the controller AI to a strategic AI. He also explained that the various soldier divisions have unique personalities. Sound Supervisor Nick Peck wrote that the process for creating sounds was to identify what was needed, create the sounds, and then integrate it into the game via programming software. He and his team researched original ''Star Wars'' sound effects, then tweaked them and integrated them into the game. Since ''Battlefront'' is an action game, Peck and his team traveled to [[Skywalker Ranch]] and spent two days creating 1200 new foley (sound) effects, a very detailed process.<ref name="DevDiaries" />

There is a playable Xbox demo on the [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|''Star Wars'' Trilogy]] [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD]] set, released on [[September 21]], [[2004]]. The demo gameplay is limited to a [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] level, and is slightly different to that of the actual game.<ref name="Demo announcement">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/publishing/news20040420.html|text=''Star Wars'' Trilogy Returns to Bookshelves}}</ref> A PS2 demo was also released with Sony's [[Wikipedia:PlayStation|PlayStation]] magazine. It also only featured the Battle of Endor.<ref name="PS 2 demo">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/games/official-playstation-magazine-vol-85/ps2-700058|text=Official PlayStation Magazine Vol. 85|work=IGN|archivedate=20160927023019}}</ref> A glitch in this demo was that the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] Wookiee Smuggler class gave orders in [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], rather than growls.

==Continuity==
===Cut content===
During development, certain content was either cut from the game entirely, or changed drastically before the final release.

*[[Seraph-class urban landspeeder|Flash Speeder]] (Appears as in Clone Wars Campaign and Trailer)
*[[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PT]] (Design differs from the Imperial version. The control pod is sleeker and features an open cockpit)
*[[All Terrain Experimental Transport|AT-XT]]
*[[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]
*[[Multi-Troop Transport/Legends|Multi-Troop Transport]]
*[[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]]
*[[BTL-S8 K-wing assault starfighter|K-wing]]
*An earlier version of the Droid Assassin used the model of an [[IG-series/Legends|IG-Series]] droid. The model was released within the Mod Tools for ''Star Wars: Battlefront''.
*A sixth unit was originally planned for the game but cut:
**A [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] unit, using the ARC Trooper model with red markings. It would have been armed with a grenade launcher, a recon droid, and health/ammo packs. A later version of the Clone Commander unit carried a blaster pistol instead of a recon droid.
**A Tactical Droid, armed with a Sonic Blaster, a Blaster Pistol, and a 'Droid Enhancer' which increased health, damage or speed dependant on which mode was selected. The Tactical Droid used the standard B-1 Battle Droid model.
**A Rebel Spy, armed with a Seeker Rifle, Remote Detonators, and a Disguise Kit which could cycle through enemy appearances. The model used for the Rebel Spy was that of a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]].
**An Imperial Officer, armed with a Blaster Pistol and a powerup dispenser for health and ammo.
*Many maps originally had different layouts to their final versions.
*Jedi heroes were originally going to be equipped with Force powers and powerful jumps.
*Generic Jedi units were to support the Republic on Geonosis. Strangely, one of the Jedi's skins looks like Darth Maul.
*A cinematic clip was going to play at the end of each battle.
Some reasons behind this may be issues of balance and/or memory. Most likely, LucasArts did not want Episode III content revealed so early (one example is the Kashyyyk: Docks map ([[Kachirho/Legends|Kachirho]]), which, at the time, could not be identified).

The Clone Commander, Imperial Officer and Bothan Spy would later become playable in ''Star Wars: Battlefront II''.

The trailer for ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' showed that players could fly Droid Starfighters and Jedi Starfighters on Kamino. This was most likely cut because gameplay was too similar to Bespin: Platforms.

===Inconsistencies with ''Star Wars'' continuity===
[[Lucasfilm]]'s Keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]], [[Leland Chee]], has stated that though technology, characters and locations in the game are C-canon, the story itself is S-canon, which means that only parts of it that appeared in other C-canon sources are [[canon]]. The story of the [[Star Wars: Battlefront II|sequel]], however, is C-canon.<ref name="Chee's blog">{{Blog|holocron|56|starwars.com forum HolocronKeeper comments archive}}</ref>

There are many major canonical inconsistencies in the ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' series. However, many of these are simply game mechanics.
*Assault droids (the CIS's heavy-weapons unit) are depicted with blue markings on their bodies while the pilot droids have no markings. This was corrected in ''Star Wars: Battlefront II''.
*In the Clone Wars campaign, ARC troopers (the Republic's heavy-weapons unit) are playable on Geonosis, and Jet Troopers are the new units (instead of ARC troopers) in The Battle of Kamino.<ref name="SW:BF" /> Previous sources indicated that ARC troopers did not participate in the Battle of Geonosis.<ref name="Republic comic">''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' comic</ref> Also, in the ''Star Wars: Battlefront II'' version of the Battle of Geonosis, both Jet Troopers and ARC Troopers are playable.
*The [[Battle of Rhen Var (Clone Wars)|first Rhen Var mission]] of the campaign takes place after the Battle of Kamino, and the mission briefing says that they are establishing a base, not defending or retaking one, and does not imply any previous battle happening.
*In the game, Imperial [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark troopers]] are not droids, but soldiers with power armor, and they give orders and cry out when they are hit as [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] do. Also, dark troopers are seen in a battle prior to their development. These inconsistencies were retconned by ''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'', which retconned them to be the [[Phase Zero dark trooper]]s, part clone trooper, part cyborg.
*In the second campaign mission, the Trade Federation wins instead of the Royal Guards.<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref>
*In the Galactic Civil War campaign, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] is present during the siege of Mos Eisley,<ref name="SW:BF" /> but in the movie he was on the Death Star while that was happening.<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> ''Star Wars'' novels state that Vader would never return to Tatooine for emotional reasons.<ref name="DL:R">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref>
*During the Battle of Hoth in campaign mode, the Rebels win<ref name="SW:BF" /> instead of the Empire.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>
*Wookiees and Imperial Darktroopers fight on Endor<ref name="SW:BF" /> even though the Darktrooper project was destroyed three years before.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

===Trooper variations===
The following are troops that change their stormtrooper or Rebel skin on certain maps:

Galactic Empire:
*Scout trooper-Hoth, Rhen Var - Snowtrooper
*Stormtrooper-Hoth, Rhen Var - Snowtrooper
*Shock trooper-Hoth, Rhen Var - Snowtrooper
*Imperial Pilot-Hoth, Rhen Var - AT-AT Pilot
*Imperial Pilot-Naboo, Endor, Tatooine, Yavin - AT-ST Pilot
*Imperial Pilot-Tatooine, Bespin, Kashyyyk - TIE Fighter Pilot

Rebel Alliance:
*Rebel Soldier - Hoth, Rhen Var - Hoth uniform
*Rebel Soldier - Endor, Yavin IV, Naboo - Forest Uniform
*Rebel Soldier - Bespin - Gray Uniform
*Rebel Vanguard - Hoth, Rhen Var - Hoth uniform
*Rebel Marksmen - Hoth, Rhen Var - Hoth uniform
*Rebel Pilot - Hoth, Rhen Var - Hoth uniform
*Wookiee - Hoth, Rhen Var - Snow covered fur

==Legacy==
''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' was released on [[November 1]], [[2005]] on the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PS2]], [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]], and [[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] platforms.<ref name="BF II press release">{{LucasArts|url=games/swbattlefrontii/indexFlash.html#GameInfo.PressRelease|text=LucasArts|nobackup=1}}</ref> The first game and the sequel were re-released on [[March 14]], [[2024]], as the ''[[Star Wars Battlefront Classic Collection]]''.<ref name="Classic Collection">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-battlefront-classic-collection|text=''Star Wars Battlefront Classic Collection'' Coming March 14}}</ref>

On the 10th of May [[2007]], [[LucasArts]] announced a [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]] exclusive addition to the ''Star Wars: Battlefront'' series, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Renegade Squadron]]'',<ref name="BF:RS announcement">{{LucasArts|url=company/release/news20070510.html|text=Star Wars Battlefront®: Renegade Squadron™ Sends PSP® System Owners to the Front}}</ref> which was released October 9, 2007.<ref name="BF:RS press release">{{LucasArts|url=company/release/news20071009.html|text=Star Wars Battlefront®: Renegade Squadron™ Now Available Exclusively for the PSP® System}}</ref> A further game exclusive to mobile platforms, ''[[Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron]]'' was released in 2009.<ref name="BF:ES press release">{{LucasArts|url=games/swbattlefrontelitesquadron/gameinfo/news/summary.html|text=Star Wars Battlefront: Elite Squadron}}</ref>

A new [[Star Wars Battlefront|version]] of ''Star Wars: Battlefront'', by [[DICE]] Studios, was announced at E3 2013, and was released on the 17th of November, 2015.<ref name="Forbes">{{WebCite|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/davidthier/2013/06/10/ea-announces-new-star-wars-battlegront-game|text=EA Announces New Star Wars: Battlefront Game|author=Thier, Dave|work=Forbes|archivedate=20190403051611}}</ref> The revived version also received a [[Star Wars Battlefront II|sequel]] in 2017.
